raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A general review of the section centrally includes checking whether the thesis, motivation, and contribution claim are framed coherently at the section level.","R1.2":"The reviewer is expected to assess whether the argument from solver limitations to architecture choice to empirical conclusion is logically consistent.","R1.3":"The section makes concrete performance and ablation claims, so review centrally includes checking whether those claims are aligned with the reported results.","R1.6":"Because the delegation asks to review a written section, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and academic register are central responsibilities.","RX.1":"Any review projection must account for uncertainty where the artifact lacks external context such as full methods, tables, or prior-work details.","RX.2":"The section contains broad claims about physical priors and other PDE systems, making overclaim avoidance an active cross-cutting responsibility.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within review-oriented responsibilities and not execute edits, rewrite the section, or perform external validation.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid endorsing potentially misleading scientific claims without adequate support.","RX.5":"The projection must remain traceable to the delegation and the provided artifact rather than unstated external assumptions."}}

rationale.R1.1: A general review of the section centrally includes checking whether the thesis, motivation, and contribution claim are framed coherently at the section level.
rationale.R1.2: The reviewer is expected to assess whether the argument from solver limitations to architecture choice to empirical conclusion is logically consistent.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes concrete performance and ablation claims, so review centrally includes checking whether those claims are aligned with the reported results.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation asks to review a written section, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and academic register are central responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: Any review projection must account for uncertainty where the artifact lacks external context such as full methods, tables, or prior-work details.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains broad claims about physical priors and other PDE systems, making overclaim avoidance an active cross-cutting responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within review-oriented responsibilities and not execute edits, rewrite the section, or perform external validation.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid endorsing potentially misleading scientific claims without adequate support.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must remain traceable to the delegation and the provided artifact rather than unstated external assumptions.
